 87	 private fun showBiometricPrompt() {
 88	 val executor = ContextCompat.getMainExecutor(this)
 89	 val biometricPrompt = AndroidXBiometricPrompt(this as FragmentActivity, executor,
 90	 object : AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback() {
 91	 override fun onAuthenticationError(errorCode: Int, errString: CharSequence) {
 92	 super.onAuthenticationError(errorCode, errString)
 93	 if (errorCode == AndroidXBiometricPrompt.ERROR_USER_CANCELED ||
 94	 errorCode == AndroidXBiometricPrompt.ERROR_CANCELED) {
 95	 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_CANCELED)
 96	 } else {
 97	 // Show error and allow retry
 98	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Authentication error: $errString"
 99	 }
 100	 }
 101	 
 102	 override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 103	 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 104	 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
 105	 }
 106	 
 107	 override fun onAuthenticationFailed() {
 108	 super.onAuthenticationFailed()
 109	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Authentication failed. Please try again."
 110	 }
 111	 })
 112	 
 113	 val promptInfo = AndroidXBiometricPrompt.PromptInfo.Builder()
 114	 .setTitle("FIDO Authentication")
 115	 .setSubtitle("Authenticate to continue with security key operation")
 116	 .setAllowedAuthenticators(BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_WEAK or BiometricManager.Authenticators.DEVICE_CREDENTIAL)
 117	 .build()
 118	 
 119	 biometricPrompt.authenticate(promptInfo)
 120	 }

 82	 private fun handleAuthenticateRequest(
 83	 context: AuthenticatorContext,
 84	 req: AuthenticationRequest
 85	 ): Pair<RequestInfo?, () -> Response.AuthenticationResponse> {
 86	 val credential = Credential.fromKeyHandle(req.keyHandle, req.application, context)
 87	 ?: throw ApduException(StatusWord.WRONG_DATA)
 88	 // CTAP2 credentials are allowed to be used via CTAP1, which greatly improves NFC usability
 89	 if (credential !is U2FCredential)
 90	 i { "Using a CTAP2 credential via CTAP1" }
 91	 val action =
 92	 if (isDummyRequest(req.application, req.challenge))
 93	 AuthenticatorAction.REGISTER_CREDENTIAL_EXCLUDED
 94	 else
 95	 AuthenticatorAction.AUTHENTICATE
 96	 return when (req.controlByte) {
 97	 AuthenticateControlByte.CHECK_ONLY -> {
 98	 throw ApduException(StatusWord.CONDITIONS_NOT_SATISFIED)
 99	 }
 100	 AuthenticateControlByte.ENFORCE_USER_PRESENCE_AND_SIGN -> {
 101	 val requestInfo =
 102	 context.makeU2fRequestInfo(action, req.application)
 103	 Pair(requestInfo) {
 104	 credential.assertU2f(
 105	 clientDataHash = req.challenge,
 106	 userPresent = true,
 107	 userVerified = false,
 108	 context = context
 109	 )
 110	 }
 111	 }
 112	 AuthenticateControlByte.DONT_ENFORCE_USER_PRESENCE_AND_SIGN -> {
 113	 i { "Processing silent Authenticate request" }
 114	 Pair(null) {
 115	 credential.assertU2f(
 116	 clientDataHash = req.challenge,
 117	 userPresent = false,
 118	 userVerified = false,
 119	 context = context
 120	 )
 121	 }
 122	 }
 123	 }
 124	 }

 126	 private fun handleVersionRequest(): Pair<Nothing?, () -> Response.VersionResponse> {
 127	 return Pair(null) { Response.VersionResponse() }
 128	 }

 167	## UI Function Stubbing
 168	
 169	All UI-dependent functions in `AuthenticatorContext.kt` have been commented out with detailed implementation hints. The test framework includes stubbed implementations that:
 170	
 171	- Return predictable values for testing
 172	- Don't require user interaction
 173	- Allow business logic testing to proceed
 174	- Maintain the same interface contracts
 175

 412	
 413	private fun signWithMasterSigningKey(vararg data: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 414	 val secretKey = androidKeystore.getSecretKey(MASTER_SIGNING_KEY_ALIAS)!!
 415	 return Mac.getInstance("HmacSHA256").run {
 416	 init(secretKey)
 417	 for (datum in data) {
 418	 update(datum)
 419	 }
 420	 doFinal()
 421	 }
 422	}
 423	
 424	fun pokeMasterSigningKey() {
 425	 GlobalScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
 426	 signWithMasterSigningKey(byteArrayOf(0))
 427	 }
 428	}
 429	
 430	private fun verifyWithMasterSigningKey(signature: ByteArray, vararg data: ByteArray): Boolean {
 431	 return MessageDigest.isEqual(signWithMasterSigningKey(*data), signature)
 432	}
 433	
 434	data class Assertion(val authenticatorData: ByteArray, val signature: ByteArray) {
 435	 init {
 436	 require(authenticatorData.size >= 32 + 1 + 4)
 437	 }
 438	}

 10	object Authenticator : Logging {
 11	 override val TAG = "U2fAuthenticator"
 12	
 13	 fun handle(context: AuthenticatorContext, req: Request): Pair<RequestInfo?, () -> Response> {
 14	 v { "<- $req" }
 15	 return when (req) {
 16	 is RegistrationRequest -> {
 17	 i { "Register request received" }
 18	 handleRegisterRequest(context, req)
 19	 }
 20	 is AuthenticationRequest -> {
 21	 i { "Authenticate request received" }
 22	 handleAuthenticateRequest(context, req)
 23	 }
 24	 is VersionRequest -> {
 25	 i { "Version request received" }
 26	 handleVersionRequest()
 27	 }
 28	 }
 29	 }

 19	 val value: T
 20	}

 23	inline class CborUnsignedInteger(override val value: ULong) :
 24	 CborBoxedValue<ULong> {
 25	 override fun writeAsCbor(out: ByteArrayOutputStream) {
 26	 value.writeAsCbor(out, MajorType.UNSIGNED_INTEGER)
 27	 }

 276	 fun getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 277	 createResidentKey: Boolean = false,
 278	 createHmacSecret: Boolean = false,
 279	 attestationChallenge: ByteArray? = null
 280	 ): Pair<String, AttestationType>? {
 281	 val useAndroidAttestation =
 282	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.getBoolean(
 283	 USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, true
 284	 )
 285	 val actualAttestationChallenge = if (useAndroidAttestation) attestationChallenge else null
 286	
 287	 if (useCachedCredential && !createResidentKey && !createHmacSecret) {
 288	 synchronized(CACHED_CREDENTIAL_ALIAS_WRITE_LOCK) {
 289	 val keyAlias =
 290	 getCachedCredentialKeyAlias(context)
 291	 if (keyAlias != null) {
 292	 setCachedCredentialKeyAlias(
 293	 context,
 294	 null
 295	 )
 296	 // attestationChallenge is ignored when using a cached key
 297	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 298	 }
 299	 }
 300	 }
 301	
 302	 val keyAlias = generateWebAuthnCredential(
 303	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 304	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 305	 attestationChallenge = actualAttestationChallenge
 306	 )
 307	 if (keyAlias == null) {
 308	 if (actualAttestationChallenge == null) {
 309	 w { "Key generation failed without attestation; giving up" }
 310	 return null
 311	 }
 312	 // We failed to generate a Keystore key, which may be due to attestation failing on this
 313	 // device. Since attestation is not essential, we fall back to self attestation for all
 314	 // future key generations.
 315	 w { "Key generation failed; falling back to self attestation in the future" }
 316	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.edit {
 317	 putBoolean(USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, false)
 318	 }
 319	 // Directly retry without attestation; explicitly set attestation challenge to null to
 320	 // prevent an infinite loop in case shared preferences are flaky.
 321	 return getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 322	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 323	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 324	 attestationChallenge = null
 325	 )
 326	 }
 327	 // For the foreseeable future, we use Basic attestation even if we managed to generate a
 328	 // credential with Android KeyStore attestation since most RPs that rely on attestation are
 329	 // not compatible with this type.
 330	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 331	 }

 344	 synchronized(COUNTERS_WRITE_LOCK) {
 345	 val current = getCounter(keyAlias) ?: return null
 346	 val new = current + 1u
 347	 setCounter(keyAlias, new)
 348	 return new
 349	 }

 564	 suspend fun setResidentCredential(
 565	 rpId: String,
 566	 userId: ByteArray,
 567	 credential: WebAuthnCredential,
 568	 userVerified: Boolean
 569	 ) {
 570	 require(userId.size <= 64)
 571	 val rpIdHash = rpId.sha256()
 572	 val encodedUserId = userId.base64()
 573	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()
 574	 val serializedCredential = try {
 575	 authenticateUserFor {
 576	 credential.serialize(userVerified)
 577	 } ?: CTAP_ERR(OperationDenied)
 578	 } catch (e: Exception) {
 579	 e { "Failed to serialize user data: $e" }
 580	 CTAP_ERR(Other)
 581	 }
 582	 v { "Storing resident credential information: {'rpId': '${rpId}', 'uid+$encodedUserId': '$serializedCredential', 'kh+$encodedKeyHandle': '$encodedUserId'}" }
 583	 getResidentKeyPrefsForRpId(rpIdHash).edit {
 584	 putString("rpId", rpId)
 585	 putString("uid+$encodedUserId", serializedCredential)
 586	 putString("kh+$encodedKeyHandle", encodedUserId)
 587	 }
 588	 i { "Resident credential stored" }
 589	 }
 590	
 591	 fun lookupAndReplaceWithResidentCredential(credential: Credential): Credential {
 592	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()
 593	 val encodedUserId =
 594	 getResidentKeyPrefsForRpId(credential.rpIdHash).getString("kh+$encodedKeyHandle", null)
 595	 ?: return credential
 596	 return (getResidentCredential(credential.rpIdHash, encodedUserId) ?: credential).also {
 597	 if (it != credential)
 598	 i { "Replaced allow list credential with resident credential" }
 599	 }
 600	 }

 171	
 172	fun generateWebAuthnCredential(
 173	 createResidentKey: Boolean = false,
 174	 createHmacSecret: Boolean = false,
 175	 attestationChallenge: ByteArray? = null
 176	): String? {
 177	 val nonce = ByteArray(32)
 178	 SecureRandom.getInstanceStrong().nextBytes(nonce)
 179	 val keyAlias = nonce.base64()
 180	 val purpose = KeyProperties.PURPOSE_SIGN
 181	 val ecParameterSpec = KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder(keyAlias, purpose).apply {
 182	 setAlgorithmParameterSpec(ECGenParameterSpec("secp256r1"))
 183	 setDigests(KeyProperties.DIGEST_SHA256)
 184	 setKeySize(256)
 185	 setAttestationChallenge(attestationChallenge)
 186	 if (createResidentKey) {
 187	 setKeyValidityStart(

 275	
 276	 fun getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 277	 createResidentKey: Boolean = false,
 278	 createHmacSecret: Boolean = false,
 279	 attestationChallenge: ByteArray? = null
 280	 ): Pair<String, AttestationType>? {
 281	 val useAndroidAttestation =
 282	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.getBoolean(
 283	 USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, true
 284	 )
 285	 val actualAttestationChallenge = if (useAndroidAttestation) attestationChallenge else null
 286	
 287	 if (useCachedCredential && !createResidentKey && !createHmacSecret) {
 288	 synchronized(CACHED_CREDENTIAL_ALIAS_WRITE_LOCK) {
 289	 val keyAlias =
 290	 getCachedCredentialKeyAlias(context)
 291	 if (keyAlias != null) {
 292	 setCachedCredentialKeyAlias(
 293	 context,
 294	 null
 295	 )
 296	 // attestationChallenge is ignored when using a cached key
 297	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 298	 }
 299	 }
 300	 }
 301	
 302	 val keyAlias = generateWebAuthnCredential(
 303	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 304	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 305	 attestationChallenge = actualAttestationChallenge
 306	 )
 307	 if (keyAlias == null) {
 308	 if (actualAttestationChallenge == null) {
 309	 w { "Key generation failed without attestation; giving up" }
 310	 return null
 311	 }
 312	 // We failed to generate a Keystore key, which may be due to attestation failing on this
 313	 // device. Since attestation is not essential, we fall back to self attestation for all
 314	 // future key generations.
 315	 w { "Key generation failed; falling back to self attestation in the future" }
 316	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.edit {
 317	 putBoolean(USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, false)
 318	 }
 319	 // Directly retry without attestation; explicitly set attestation challenge to null to
 320	 // prevent an infinite loop in case shared preferences are flaky.
 321	 return getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 322	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 323	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 324	 attestationChallenge = null
 325	 )
 326	 }
 327	 // For the foreseeable future, we use Basic attestation even if we managed to generate a
 328	 // credential with Android KeyStore attestation since most RPs that rely on attestation are
 329	 // not compatible with this type.
 330	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 331	 }
 332	
 333	 fun refreshCachedWebAuthnCredentialIfNecessary() {
 334	 refreshCachedWebAuthnCredentialIfNecessary(context)
 335	 }

 563	
 564	 suspend fun setResidentCredential(
 565	 rpId: String,
 566	 userId: ByteArray,
 567	 credential: WebAuthnCredential,
 568	 userVerified: Boolean
 569	 ) {
 570	 require(userId.size <= 64)
 571	 val rpIdHash = rpId.sha256()
 572	 val encodedUserId = userId.base64()
 573	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()
 574	 val serializedCredential = try {
 575	 authenticateUserFor {
 576	 credential.serialize(userVerified)
 577	 } ?: CTAP_ERR(OperationDenied)
 578	 } catch (e: Exception) {
 579	 e { "Failed to serialize user data: $e" }
 580	 CTAP_ERR(Other)
 581	 }

 619	 return prefs.all.keys.filter { it.startsWith("uid+") }
 620	 .map { it.substring(4) }.toList()
 621	 }

 289	 val keyAlias =
 290	 getCachedCredentialKeyAlias(context)
 291	 if (keyAlias != null) {
 292	 setCachedCredentialKeyAlias(
 293	 context,
 294	 null
 295	 )
 296	 // attestationChallenge is ignored when using a cached key
 297	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 298	 }
 299	 }
 300	 }

 319	 // Directly retry without attestation; explicitly set attestation challenge to null to
 320	 // prevent an infinite loop in case shared preferences are flaky.
 321	 return getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 322	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 323	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 324	 attestationChallenge = null
 325	 )
 326	 }
 327	 // For the foreseeable future, we use Basic attestation even if we managed to generate a
 328	 // credential with Android KeyStore attestation since most RPs that rely on attestation are
 329	 // not compatible with this type.
 330	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 331	 }

 72	 override fun processCommandApdu(commandApdu: ByteArray, extras: Bundle?): ByteArray? {
 73	 v { "<- ${commandApdu.toHexString()}" }
 74	 val rawCommandApdu = commandApdu.asUByteArray()
 75	 if (SELECT_FIDO_APDUS.any { rawCommandApdu.contentEquals(it) }) {
 76	 isSelected = true
 77	 initiateVibration()
 78	 lastResponseApdu = null
 79	 chainedRequestBuffer = ubyteArrayOf()
 80	 onDeactivatedMessage = null
 81	 return (CTAP_VERSION_STRING_BYTES + StatusWord.NO_ERROR.value).asByteArray().also {
 82	 v { "-> ${it.toHexString()}" }
 83	 }
 84	 }
 85	 if (!isSelected) {
 86	 return StatusWord.CONDITIONS_NOT_SATISFIED.value.asByteArray().also {
 87	 v { "-> ${it.toHexString()}" }
 88	 }
 89	 }
 90	 val apdu = CommandApdu(rawCommandApdu)
 91	 if (apdu.headerEquals(DESELECT_FIDO_APDU_HEADER)) {
 92	 isSelected = false
 93	 return StatusWord.NO_ERROR.value.asByteArray().also {
 94	 v { "-> ${it.toHexString()}" }
 95	 }
 96	 }
 97	 launch {
 98	 val response = try {
 99	 handleRequestApdu(apdu)
 100	 } catch (error: ApduException) {
 101	 d {
 102	 "Unable to handle APDU with header ${rawCommandApdu.asByteArray().sliceArray(0..3).toHexString()}; returned ${error.statusWord}"
 103	 }
 104	 lastResponseApdu = null
 105	 error.statusWord.value
 106	 }
 107	 v { "-> ${response.asByteArray().toHexString()}" }
 108	 sendResponseApdu(response.asByteArray())
 109	 }
 110	 resetVibrationTimeout()
 111	 return null
 112	 }
...
 124	 lastResponseApdu = when {
 125	 apdu.headerEquals(NFCCTAP_MSG_CHAINING_HEADER) -> {
 126	 if (chainedRequestBuffer.size + apdu.data.size > 65536)
 127	 throw ApduException(StatusWord.WRONG_LENGTH)
 128	 chainedRequestBuffer += apdu.data
 129	 ResponseApdu(
 130	 ubyteArrayOf(),
 131	 StatusWord.NO_ERROR
 132	 )
 133	 }
 134	 NFCCTAP_MSG_HEADERS.any { apdu.headerEquals(it) } -> {
 135	 if (chainedRequestBuffer.size + apdu.data.size > 65536)
 136	 throw ApduException(StatusWord.WRONG_LENGTH)
 137	 val ctapRawRequest = (chainedRequestBuffer + apdu.data).asByteArray()
 138	 val ctapRawResponse = Authenticator.handle(authenticatorContext, ctapRawRequest)
 139	 ResponseApdu(
 140	 ctapRawResponse.asUByteArray(),
 141	 StatusWord.NO_ERROR
 142	 )
 143	 }
 144	 else -> {
 145	 // Everything else is either a U2F (CTAP1) request or an error.
 146	 val u2fRequest = U2fRequest.parse(apdu)
 147	 val (requestInfo, cont) = U2fAuthenticator.handle(authenticatorContext, u2fRequest)
 148	 if (requestInfo != null)
 149	 authenticatorContext.notifyUser(requestInfo)
 150	 // User presence is always certain via NFC, always continue
 151	 val u2fResponse = cont()
 152	 Logging.v(U2fAuthenticator.TAG) { "-> $u2fResponse" }
 153	 ResponseApdu(
 154	 u2fResponse.data,
 155	 u2fResponse.statusWord
 156	 )
 157	 }
 158	 }
 159	 if (!apdu.headerEquals(NFCCTAP_MSG_CHAINING_HEADER))
 160	 chainedRequestBuffer = ubyteArrayOf()
 161	 return lastResponseApdu!!.next(apdu.le)
 162	 }

 210	 AuthenticatorContext(applicationContext, isHidTransport = false) {
 211	
 212	 override fun notifyUser(info: RequestInfo) {
 213	 onDeactivatedMessage = info.successMessage
 214	 }
 215	
 216	 override fun handleSpecialStatus(specialStatus: AuthenticatorSpecialStatus) {
 217	 when (specialStatus) {
 218	 RESET -> {
 219	 onDeactivatedMessage = getString(R.string.reset_via_bluetooth_only)
 220	 }
 221	 USER_NOT_AUTHENTICATED -> {
 222	 onDeactivatedMessage = getString(R.string.verify_screen_lock_and_retry)
 223	 confirmDeviceCredentialOnDeactivated = true
 224	 }
 225	 }
 226	 }
 227	
 228	 override suspend fun confirmRequestWithUser(info: RequestInfo): Boolean {
 229	 // User presence is always certain with NFC transport.
 230	 return true
 231	 }

 356	 suspend fun <T> authenticateUserFor(block: () -> T): T? {
 357	 // Confirm device credential and retry if block throws a UserNotAuthenticatedException.
 358	 return try {
 359	 block()
 360	 } catch (e1: UserNotAuthenticatedException) {
 361	 confirmDeviceCredentialInternal(updateAuthenticatorStatus = true)
 362	 try {
 363	 block()
 364	 } catch (e2: UserNotAuthenticatedException) {
 365	 null
 366	 }
 367	 }
 368	 }

 101	@ExperimentalUnsignedTypes
 102	fun createU2fSelfAttestationCert(credential: U2FCredential): ByteArray {
 103	 val certBody = REFERENCE_ATTESTATION_CERT_TEMPLATE + credential.u2fPublicKeyRepresentation!!
 104	 val certSignaturePayload = credential.sign(certBody)
 105	 val certSignaturePrefix = ubyteArrayOf(0x03u, (certSignaturePayload.size + 1).toUByte(), 0x00u).toByteArray()
 106	 val certSignature = REFERENCE_ATTESTATION_CERT_SIGNATURE_TEMPLATE + certSignaturePrefix + certSignaturePayload
 107	 val certHeader = ubyteArrayOf(0x30u, 0x82u).toByteArray() + (certBody.size + certSignature.size).toUShort().bytes()
 108	 return certHeader + certBody + certSignature
 109	}

 192	 )

 932	 )

 934	 factory.getKeySpec(key, KeyInfo::class.java) as KeyInfo

 946	fun checkAllKeysInHardware(): Boolean {
 947	 for (alias in androidKeystore.aliases()) {
 948	 val keyInfo = getKeyInfo(alias) ?: return false
 949	 if (!keyInfo.isInsideSecureHardware || keyInfo.origin != KeyProperties.ORIGIN_GENERATED) {
 950	 return false
 951	 }
 952	 }
 953	 return true
 954	}

 276	 fun getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 277	 createResidentKey: Boolean = false,
 278	 createHmacSecret: Boolean = false,
 279	 attestationChallenge: ByteArray? = null
 280	 ): Pair<String, AttestationType>? {
 281	 val useAndroidAttestation =
 282	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.getBoolean(
 283	 USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, true
 284	 )
 285	 val actualAttestationChallenge = if (useAndroidAttestation) attestationChallenge else null
 286	
 287	 if (useCachedCredential && !createResidentKey && !createHmacSecret) {
 288	 synchronized(CACHED_CREDENTIAL_ALIAS_WRITE_LOCK) {
 289	 val keyAlias =
 290	 getCachedCredentialKeyAlias(context)
 291	 if (keyAlias != null) {
 292	 setCachedCredentialKeyAlias(
 293	 context,
 294	 null
 295	 )
 296	 // attestationChallenge is ignored when using a cached key
 297	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 298	 }
 299	 }
 300	 }
 301	
 302	 val keyAlias = generateWebAuthnCredential(
 303	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 304	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 305	 attestationChallenge = actualAttestationChallenge
 306	 )
 307	 if (keyAlias == null) {
 308	 if (actualAttestationChallenge == null) {
 309	 w { "Key generation failed without attestation; giving up" }
 310	 return null
 311	 }
 312	 // We failed to generate a Keystore key, which may be due to attestation failing on this
 313	 // device. Since attestation is not essential, we fall back to self attestation for all
 314	 // future key generations.
 315	 w { "Key generation failed; falling back to self attestation in the future" }
 316	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.edit {
 317	 putBoolean(USE_ANDROID_ATTESTATION_PREFERENCE_KEY, false)
 318	 }
 319	 // Directly retry without attestation; explicitly set attestation challenge to null to
 320	 // prevent an infinite loop in case shared preferences are flaky.
 321	 return getOrCreateFreshWebAuthnCredential(
 322	 createResidentKey = createResidentKey,
 323	 createHmacSecret = createHmacSecret,
 324	 attestationChallenge = null
 325	 )
 326	 }
 327	 // For the foreseeable future, we use Basic attestation even if we managed to generate a
 328	 // credential with Android KeyStore attestation since most RPs that rely on attestation are
 329	 // not compatible with this type.
 330	 return Pair(keyAlias, AttestationType.BASIC)
 331	 }

 564	 suspend fun setResidentCredential(
 565	 rpId: String,
 566	 userId: ByteArray,
 567	 credential: WebAuthnCredential,
 568	 userVerified: Boolean
 569	 ) {
 570	 require(userId.size <= 64)
 571	 val rpIdHash = rpId.sha256()
 572	 val encodedUserId = userId.base64()
 573	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()
 574	 val serializedCredential = try {
 575	 authenticateUserFor {
 576	 credential.serialize(userVerified)
 577	 } ?: CTAP_ERR(OperationDenied)
 578	 } catch (e: Exception) {
 579	 e { "Failed to serialize user data: $e" }
 580	 CTAP_ERR(Other)
 581	 }
 582	 v { "Storing resident credential information: {'rpId': '${rpId}', 'uid+$encodedUserId': '$serializedCredential', 'kh+$encodedKeyHandle': '$encodedUserId'}" }
 583	 getResidentKeyPrefsForRpId(rpIdHash).edit {
 584	 putString("rpId", rpId)
 585	 putString("uid+$encodedUserId", serializedCredential)
 586	 putString("kh+$encodedKeyHandle", encodedUserId)
 587	 }
 588	 i { "Resident credential stored" }
 589	 }

 591	 fun lookupAndReplaceWithResidentCredential(credential: Credential): Credential {
 592	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()

 594	 getResidentKeyPrefsForRpId(credential.rpIdHash).getString("kh+$encodedKeyHandle", null)

 596	 return (getResidentCredential(credential.rpIdHash, encodedUserId) ?: credential).also {
 597	 if (it != credential)
 598	 i { "Replaced allow list credential with resident credential" }
 599	 }
 600	 }

 789	 fun deleteResidentCredential(context: Context, credential: WebAuthnCredential) {
 790	 check(credential.userId != null)
 791	 val rpIdHashString = credential.rpIdHash.base64()
 792	 val encodedUserId = credential.userId.base64()
 793	 val encodedKeyHandle = credential.keyHandle.base64()
 794	 val rpPrefs = getResidentKeyPrefsForRpId(context, credential.rpIdHash)
 795	 rpPrefs.edit {
 796	 remove("uid+$encodedUserId")
 797	 remove("kh+$encodedKeyHandle")
 798	 }
 799	 if (rpPrefs.all.none { it.key.startsWith("uid+") || it.key.startsWith("kh+") }) {
 800	 // This was the last resident credential for this RP, delete its record.
 801	 context.deleteSharedPreferences(RESIDENT_KEY_PREFERENCE_FILE_PREFIX + rpIdHashString)
 802	 context.sharedPreferences(RESIDENT_KEY_RP_ID_HASHES_FILE).edit {
 803	 remove(rpIdHashString)
 804	 }
 805	 }
 806	 }

 810	 context: Context,

 814	 v { "Writing RP ID hash entry for $rpIdHashString" }

 854	 v { "Deserialized ${it.second.size} credentials for '$rpId'" }
 855	 }
 856	 }.toMap().also {
 857	 d { "Found resident credentials for ${it.size} RPs with ${unknownSiteCounter - 1} unknown sites" }
 858	 }

 96	 private fun showBiometricPrompt() {
 97	 Timber.d("showBiometricPrompt() called")
 98	
 99	 val executor = ContextCompat.getMainExecutor(this)
 100	 val biometricPrompt = AndroidXBiometricPrompt(this as FragmentActivity, executor,
 101	 object : AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback() {
 102	 override fun onAuthenticationError(errorCode: Int, errString: CharSequence) {
 103	 super.onAuthenticationError(errorCode, errString)
 104	 Timber.e("Authentication error: code=$errorCode, message=$errString")
 105	
 106	 if (errorCode == AndroidXBiometricPrompt.ERROR_USER_CANCELED ||
 107	 errorCode == AndroidXBiometricPrompt.ERROR_CANCELED) {
 108	 Timber.d("User canceled authentication")
 109	 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_CANCELED)
 110	 } else {
 111	 // Show error and allow retry
 112	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Authentication error: $errString"
 113	 }
 114	 }
 115	
 116	 override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 117	 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 118	 Timber.d("Authentication succeeded! Finishing with RESULT_OK")
 119	 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
 120	 }
 121	
 122	 override fun onAuthenticationFailed() {
 123	 super.onAuthenticationFailed()
 124	 Timber.w("Authentication failed - user can retry")
 125	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Authentication failed. Please try again."
 126	 }
 127	 })
 128	 
 129	 val promptInfo = AndroidXBiometricPrompt.PromptInfo.Builder()
 130	 .setTitle("FIDO Authentication")

 73	 private fun checkBiometricAvailability() {
 74	 val biometricManager = BiometricManager.from(this)
 75	 when (biometricManager.canAuthenticate(BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_WEAK or BiometricManager.Authenticators.DEVICE_CREDENTIAL)) {
 76	 BiometricManager.BIOMETRIC_SUCCESS -> {
 77	 // Biometric authentication is available
 78	 binding.messageTextView.text = getString(R.string.confirm_device_credential_message)
 79	 }
 80	 BiometricManager.BIOMETRIC_ERROR_NO_HARDWARE -> {
 81	 binding.messageTextView.text = "No biometric hardware available. Please use device PIN/password."
 82	 }
 83	 BiometricManager.BIOMETRIC_ERROR_HW_UNAVAILABLE -> {
 84	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Biometric hardware unavailable. Please use device PIN/password."
 85	 }
 86	 BiometricManager.BIOMETRIC_ERROR_NONE_ENROLLED -> {
 87	 binding.messageTextView.text = "No biometric credentials enrolled. Please use device PIN/password."
 88	 }
 89	 else -> {
 90	 binding.messageTextView.text = "Authentication not available. Please set up device security."
 91	 binding.authenticateButton.isEnabled = false
 92	 }
 93	 }
 94	 }

 51	fun KeyStore.getSecretKey(keyAlias: String): SecretKey? {
 52	 return if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.P) {
 53	 getKey(keyAlias, null) as? SecretKey
 54	 } else {
 55	 (getEntry(keyAlias, null) as? KeyStore.SecretKeyEntry)?.secretKey
 56	 }
 57	}

 171	
 172	fun generateWebAuthnCredential(
 173	 createResidentKey: Boolean = false,
 174	 createHmacSecret: Boolean = false,
 175	 attestationChallenge: ByteArray? = null
 176	): String? {
 177	 val nonce = ByteArray(32)
 178	 SecureRandom.getInstanceStrong().nextBytes(nonce)
 179	 val keyAlias = nonce.base64()
 180	 val purpose = KeyProperties.PURPOSE_SIGN
 181	 val ecParameterSpec = KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder(keyAlias, purpose).apply {
 182	 setAlgorithmParameterSpec(ECGenParameterSpec("secp256r1"))
 183	 setDigests(KeyProperties.DIGEST_SHA256)
 184	 setKeySize(256)
 185	 setAttestationChallenge(attestationChallenge)
 186	 if (createResidentKey) {
 187	 setKeyValidityStart(
 188	 Date.from(
 189	 Instant.now().minusSeconds(
 190	 RESIDENT_KEY_CREATION_TIME_SHIFT_S
 191	 )
 192	 )
 193	 )
 194	 }
 195	 }

 221	private fun getOrCreateUserInfoEncryptionKeyIfNecessary(): SecretKey? {
 222	 if (isValidKeyAlias(USER_INFO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_ALIAS))
 223	 return androidKeystore.getSecretKey(USER_INFO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_ALIAS)
 224	 val purposes = KeyProperties.PURPOSE_DECRYPT or KeyProperties.PURPOSE_ENCRYPT
 225	 val parameterSpec =
 226	 KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder(USER_INFO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_ALIAS, purposes).apply {
 227	 setBlockModes(KeyProperties.BLOCK_MODE_GCM)
 228	 setEncryptionPaddings(KeyProperties.ENCRYPTION_PADDING_NONE)
 229	 setUserAuthenticationRequired(true)
 230	 // Long enough that users should not be asked to confirm their device credentials
 231	 // because of it in normal usage scenarios, but short enough not to trigger undocumented
 232	 // Keystore failures (at around a week).
 233	 setUserAuthenticationValidityDurationSeconds(3 * 24 * 60 * 60)
 234	 }
 235	 return generateSymmetricKey(
 236	 KeyProperties.KEY_ALGORITHM_AES,
 237	 parameterSpec
 238	 ).also {
 239	 if (it == null)
 240	 e(TAG) { "Failed to initialize user info encryption key" }
 241	 }
 242	}

 244	private fun recreateInvalidatedUserInfoEncryptionKey(): SecretKey? {
 245	 i(TAG) { "User info encryption was no longer valid and has been recreated" }
 246	 deleteKey(USER_INFO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_ALIAS)
 247	 return getOrCreateUserInfoEncryptionKeyIfNecessary()
 248	}
 249	
 250	fun encryptWithUserInfoEncryptionKey(data: ByteArray): ByteArray? {
 251	 val secretKey = try {
 252	 getOrCreateUserInfoEncryptionKeyIfNecessary()
 253	 } catch (e: UnrecoverableEntryException) {
 254	 recreateInvalidatedUserInfoEncryptionKey()
 255	 } ?: return null
 256	 val cipher = try {
 257	 Cipher.getInstance(AES_GCM_NO_PADDING).apply {
 258	 init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, secretKey)
 259	 }
 260	 } catch (e: KeyPermanentlyInvalidatedException) {
 261	 val newSecretKey = recreateInvalidatedUserInfoEncryptionKey()
 262	 ?: return null
 263	 Cipher.getInstance(AES_GCM_NO_PADDING).apply {
 264	 init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, newSecretKey)
 265	 }
 266	 }
 267	 val iv = cipher.iv
 268	 check(iv.size < 256)
 269	 val cipherText = cipher.doFinal(data)
 270	 // We assume that the length of the auth tag is 16, which is the default.
 271	 check(cipherText.size == data.size + 16)
 272	 return byteArrayOf(iv.size.toByte()) + iv + cipherText
 273	}
 274	
 275	fun decryptWithUserInfoEncryptionKey(data: ByteArray): ByteArray? {
 276	 if (data.isEmpty()) {
 277	 e(TAG) { "Failed to decrypt user info: Stored data is empty" }
 278	 return null
 279	 }
 280	 val ivSize = data[0]
 281	 if (data.size < 1 + ivSize) {
 282	 e(TAG) { "Failed to decrypt user info: Stored IV size is invalid." }
 283	 return null
 284	 }

 305	fun armUserVerificationFuse(context: Context) {
 306	 if (getUserVerificationState(context) != false)
 307	 return
 308	
 309	 context.defaultSharedPreferences.edit {
 310	 putBoolean(FUSE_CREATED_PREFERENCE_KEY, true)
 311	 }
 312	 val parameterSpec =
 313	 KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder(USER_VERIFICATION_FUSE_KEY_ALIAS, KeyProperties.PURPOSE_SIGN)
 314	 .apply {
 315	 setDigests(KeyProperties.DIGEST_SHA256)
 316	 setKeySize(256)
 317	 setUserAuthenticationRequired(true)
 318	 setUserAuthenticationValidityDurationSeconds(USER_VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT_S)
 319	 setUserAuthenticationValidWhileOnBody(true)
 320	 }
 321	 // No need to handle UserNotAuthenticatedException here since the user confirmed their device
 322	 // credential just before this function was called.
 323	 if (generateSymmetricKey(
 324	 KeyProperties.KEY_ALGORITHM_HMAC_SHA256,
 325	 parameterSpec
 326	 ) != null
 327	 )
 328	 i(TAG) { "Armed user verification fuse" }
 329	 else
 330	 e(TAG) { "Failed to arm user verification fuse" }
 331	}

 452	 )

 500	 @ExperimentalUnsignedTypes
 501	 private fun assert(
 502	 clientDataHash: ByteArray,
 503	 extensionOutputs: CborValue?,
 504	 userPresent: Boolean,
 505	 userVerified: Boolean,
 506	 context: AuthenticatorContext
 507	 ): Assertion {
 508	 val flags = (if (extensionOutputs != null) FLAGS_ED_INCLUDED else 0) or
 509	 (if (userPresent) FLAGS_USER_PRESENT else 0) or
 510	 (if (userVerified) FLAGS_USER_VERIFIED else 0)
 511	 // Either we just created the credential and the counter, or its existence has been checked
 512	 // in fromKeyHandle.
 513	 val counter = context.atomicallyIncrementAndGetCounter(keyAlias)!!
 514	 val authenticatorData =
 515	 rpIdHash + flags.bytes() + counter.bytes() + (extensionOutputs?.toCbor()
 516	 ?: byteArrayOf())
 517	 val signature = sign(authenticatorData, clientDataHash)
 518	 return Assertion(authenticatorData, signature)
 519	 }

 676	@ExperimentalUnsignedTypes
 677	class WebAuthnCredential(
 678	 override val keyAlias: String,
 679	 override val rpIdHash: ByteArray,
 680	 val rpName: String? = null,
 681	 val userId: ByteArray? = null,
 682	 userDisplayName: String? = null,
 683	 userName: String? = null,
 684	 userIcon: String? = null,
 685	 val encryptedUserMap: ByteArray? = null
 686	) : Credential() {
 687	
 688	 init {
 689	 require(keyAlias.base64()?.size == 32)
 690	 require(rpIdHash.size == 32)
 691	 require(rpName == null || rpName.length <= 64)
 692	 }
 693	
 694	 var userDisplayName = userDisplayName
 695	 private set
 696	 var userName = userName
 697	 private set
 698	 var userIcon = userIcon
 699	 private set
 700	
 701	 override fun toKeyHandle(): ByteArray {
 702	 val nonce = keyAlias.base64()!!
 703	 val keyHandleData = nonce + byteArrayOf(0) + (rpName?.toByteArray() ?: byteArrayOf())
 704	 val keyHandleSignature =
 705	 signWithMasterSigningKey(
 706	 rpIdHash,
 707	 keyHandleData
 708	 )
 709	 return keyHandleData + keyHandleSignature
 710	 }
 711	
 712	 fun unlockUserInfoIfNecessary() {
 713	 if (!isResident || encryptedUserMap == null)
 714	 return
 715	 val rawUserMap =
 716	 decryptWithUserInfoEncryptionKey(
 717	 encryptedUserMap
 718	 )
 719	 if (rawUserMap != null) {
 720	 val userMap = fromCborToEnd(rawUserMap)
 721	 userDisplayName = userMap.getOptional("displayName")?.unbox()
 722	 userName = userMap.getOptional("name")?.unbox()
 723	 userIcon = userMap.getOptional("icon")?.unbox()
 724	 }
 725	 }

 727	 fun serialize(userVerified: Boolean): String {
 728	 check(isResident)
 729	 // These fields are always present for resident credentials we just created.
 730	 check(userId != null)
 731	 check(userName != null)
 732	 return CborTextStringMap(mutableMapOf<String, CborValue>(
 733	 "keyAlias" to CborTextString(keyAlias),
 734	 "userId" to CborByteString(userId)
 735	 ).also { map ->
 736	 rpName?.let { map["rpName"] = CborTextString(it) }
 737	 // Personal information is stored only if user verification is enabled, which is allowed
 738	 // by the spec and works around the potential privacy leak of having someone else
 739	 // configure user verification to get access to this information.
 740	 if (userVerified) {
 741	 val userMap = mutableMapOf(
 742	 "name" to CborTextString(userName!!)
 743	 )

Path: README.md
 1	# WearAuthn (Beta)
 2	
 3	[<img src="_assets/screen_1.png" alt="Get it on Google Play" width=300px />](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=me.henneke.wearauthn.authenticator)
 4	[<img src="https://play.google.com/intl/en_us/badges/static/images/badges/en_badge_web_generic.png" alt="Get it on Google Play" width=400px />](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=me.henneke.wearauthn.authenticator)
 5	
 6	Use your watch as a security key via Bluetooth and NFC and confirm sign-ins with a single tap on your watch. With support for the "resident key" feature of the FIDO2 standard, you can even log in to certain sites simply by confirming your watch's screen lock, without having to enter a username or password.
...
 30	
 31	Traditionally, security keys take the form of USB sticks, but there are also models that can be used via NFC, Bluetooth or Lightning. But many computers and phones even come with security key functionality built-in: Windows Hello, macOS Touch ID (with Chrome) or your Android 7+ phone can all be used with sites asking for a security key. Unfortunately, Wear OS watches could so far not be used as security keys, even though many of them are equipped with hardware facilities for cryptographic keys used for Google Pay. This is where WearAuthn comes in.
 32	
 33	## Installation
 34	
 35	In addition to the Google Play Store, you can also download the latest [nightly build](https://github.com/fmeum/WearAuthn/releases/tag/nightly).
 36	
 37	## Usage
 38	
 39	Watch a demo:
 40	
 41	[![WebAuthn demo video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/yghpblun8sM/0.jpg)](https://youtu.be/yghpblun8sM)
 42	
 43	WearAuthn can be used as a security key for your devices either via Bluetooth or Near Field Communication (NFC). All Wear OS watches are equipped with Bluetooth, but only the ones that come with Google Pay will have support for NFC.
 44	
 45	### Bluetooth
 46	
 47	In order to use WearAuthn with a Bluetooth-capable device, simply pair the device once from your watch's Bluetooth settings. Afterwards, launch WearAuthn and tap on your device in the list of paired Bluetooth devices. Once WearAuthn is "attached", the device will behave as if a USB security key were attached to it.
 48	
 49	### NFC
...
 128	
 129	- [`authenticator`](authenticator) (Wear OS app module): watch app that offers U2F/FIDO2 via Bluetooth HID and NFC; contains the following packages below `me.henneke.wearauthn`:
 130	 - [`bthid`](authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/bthid): Bluetooth HID device emulation for API levels 28+ (thanks to @ginkage and his [WearMouse](https://github.com/ginkage/wearmouse))
 131	 - [`complication`](authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/complication): Watch face complication that starts WearAuthn and connects to a preset device (available as an in-app purchase in the Play Store version)
 132	 - [`fido`](authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/fido): Implementation of the U2F and FIDO2 authenticator functionality
...
 139	 - [`ui`](authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/ui): Authenticator UI classes, such as the timed confirmation dialog and device list
 140	- [`companion`](companion) (Android app module): phone app with in-app purchase for unlocking the watch face complication
 141	- [`metadata`](metadata): FIDO Authenticator Metadata for WearAuthn, which describes its features and can be used to run conformance tests
 142	
 143	<b id="footnote">1:</b> The open source version of WearAuthn differs from the Play Store in that it does not run on watches on API levels below 28. This is because support for Bluetooth HID device emulation is only available via reflection on these older versions of Wear OS/Android.
...
Path: authenticator/src/main/res/raw/privacy_policy
 1	# WearAuthn Privacy Policy
 2	
 3	The Wear OS app [WearAuthn](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=me.henneke.wearauthn.authenticator) is a U2F/FIDO2-compliant security key. It can be used to register with and, afterwards, log in to websites that use WebAuthn for second-factor or single-factor login.
 4	
 5	WearAuthn only exchanges data with previously paired and connected Bluetooth devices and via NFC, and only to the extent of the [CTAP 2.1 specification](https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido2/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-rd-20191217.html) for FIDO2-compliant authenticators. In particular, WearAuthn itself does not transmit (or receive) data over the internet and does not contain trackers or advertisement of any kind.
 6	
 7	WearAuthn can be operated in second-factor or passwordless mode. In second-factor mode, WearAuthn will
 8	
 9	* only after acquiring consent from the user, create a random, uniquely identifying public/private key pair on the watch, store the private key pair in secure OS facilities, and share the public key with the device that requested so via Bluetooth or NFC. This public key will be forwarded to the website the user is currently registering with by that device.

Path: PRIVACY.md
 1	# WearAuthn Privacy Policy
 2	
 3	The Wear OS app [WearAuthn](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=me.henneke.wearauthn.authenticator) is a U2F/FIDO2-compliant security key. It can be used to register with and, afterwards, log in to websites that use WebAuthn for second-factor or single-factor login.
 4	
 5	WearAuthn only exchanges data with previously paired and connected Bluetooth devices and via NFC, and only to the extent of the [CTAP 2.1 specification](https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido2/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-rd-20191217.html) for FIDO2-compliant authenticators. In particular, WearAuthn itself does not transmit (or receive) data over the internet and does not contain trackers or advertisement of any kind.

 4	
 5	WearAuthn tauscht Daten nur mit vorher gekoppelten und verbundenen Bluetooth-Geräten und per NFC aus. Beides geschieht ausschließlich im Rahmen der [CTAP 2.1-Spezifikation](https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido2/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-rd-20191217.html) für FIDO2-kompatible Sicherheitsschlüssel. Inbesondere überträgt (oder empfängt) WearAuthn selbst keine Daten über das Internet und enhält keine "Tracker" oder Werbund in jedweder Form.
 6	
 7	WearAuthn kann im Zwei-Faktor- oder im passwortlosen Modus betrieben werden. Im Zwei-Faktor-Modus wird WearAuthn
 8	
 9	* nur mit expliziter Zustimmung des Nutzers ein zufällig erzeugtes und eindeutig identifizierendes Schlüsselpaar auf der Uhr erstellen, von dem der private Schlüssel in sicheren Betriebssystemelementen gespeichert und der öffentliche Schlüssel per Bluetooth oder NFC an das anfordernde Gerät übertragen wird.  92	 println("Error loading status: ${e.message}")
 93	 }
 94	 }

 16	 suspend fun handle(context: AuthenticatorContext, rawRequest: ByteArray): ByteArray {

 24	 val rawCommand = rawRequestIterator.next()
 25	 val command = RequestCommand.fromByte(rawCommand)
 26	 ?: CTAP_ERR(InvalidCommand, "Unsupported command: $rawCommand")
 27	 when (command) {
 28	 RequestCommand.MakeCredential -> {
 29	 i { "MakeCredential called" }
 30	 val params = fromCborToEnd(rawRequestIterator)
 31	 ?: CTAP_ERR(InvalidCbor, "Invalid CBOR in MakeCredential request")

 218	 attestationChallenge = clientDataHash

1. Calls `finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)` in the `onAuthenticationSucceeded` callback
2. This method sends the success result back to the caller via the `ResultReceiver`
3. Then calls `finish()` to close the activity

<augment_code_snippet path="authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/ui/ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity.kt" mode="EXCERPT">
```kotlin
override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
}

private fun finishWithResult(resultCode: Int) {
 resultReceiver?.send(resultCode, Bundle())
 finish()
}
```
</augment_code_snippet>

This success result is then used by the `AuthenticatorContext` to continue with the FIDO authentication flow, allowing the security key operation to proceed after the user has been verified.

1. Calls `finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)` in the `onAuthenticationSucceeded` callback
2. This method sends the success result back to the caller via the `ResultReceiver`
3. Then calls `finish()` to close the activity

````kotlin path=authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/ui/ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity.kt mode=EXCERPT
override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
}

private fun finishWithResult(resultCode: Int) {
 resultReceiver?.send(resultCode, Bundle())
 finish()
}
````

This success result is then used by the `AuthenticatorContext` to continue with the FIDO authentication flow, allowing the security key operation to proceed after the user has been verified.

after that i cant see any logs ","rich_text_json_repr":{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"2025-07-18 11:35:03.928 26780-26834 BiometricPrompt me.henneke.wearauthn.phone.nightly I onAuthenticationSucceeded: 1"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"text","text":"after that i cant see any logs "}]}]},"mentioned_items":[],"structured_output_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":0,"content":"Based on the log you've shared, I can see the biometric authentication succeeded at 11:35:03.928, but there are no explicit logs from your application showing what happens immediately after that success.

However, we can infer what's happening from the subsequent system logs:

1. At 11:35:03.959 - The system is handling UI elements for `MainActivity` (navigation bar and status bar)
2. At 11:35:03.961 - The `ViewRoot` for `MainActivity` is being updated
3. At 11:35:03.993-11:35:03.999 - The `ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity` is being stopped and its window is being destroyed

This sequence indicates that after biometric authentication succeeds:

1. The `onAuthenticationSucceeded` callback is triggered
2. Your code calls `finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)` which sends the result to the caller
3. The activity finishes and control returns to `MainActivity`

To better understand what's happening in your application after authentication, you should add logging in these key places:

<augment_code_snippet path="authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/ui/ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity.kt" mode="EDIT">
```kotlin
override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 Log.i(TAG, "Authentication succeeded, sending RESULT_OK to caller")
 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
}

private fun finishWithResult(resultCode: Int) {
 Log.i(TAG, "Sending result code: $resultCode to ResultReceiver")
 resultReceiver?.send(resultCode, Bundle())
 finish()
}
```
</augment_code_snippet>

Also, add logging in the `ResultReceiver` implementation in `AuthenticatorContext.kt` where you handle the authentication result:

<augment_code_snippet path="authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/fido/context/AuthenticatorContext.kt" mode="EDIT">
```kotlin
// In the confirmDeviceCredentialInternal method
val intent = Intent(context, ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity::class.java).apply {
 putExtra(
 EXTRA_CONFIRM_DEVICE_CREDENTIAL_RECEIVER,
 object : ResultReceiver(Handler()) {
 override fun onReceiveResult(resultCode: Int, resultData: Bundle?) {
 Log.i(TAG, "Received authentication result: $resultCode")
 // Process the result here
 continuation.resume(null)
 }
 })
 // ...
}
```
</augment_code_snippet>

This will help you track the flow of execution after biometric authentication succeeds.

after that i cant see any logs "}},{"id":1,"type":4,"ide_state_node":{"workspaceFolders":[{"repositoryRoot":"C:/Users/kucharovic/AndroidStudioProjects/wearauth/WearAuthn","folderRoot":"C:/Users/kucharovic/AndroidStudioProjects/wearauth/WearAuthn"}],"workspaceFoldersUnchanged":false}}],"timestamp":"2025-07-18T09:40:22.340Z","response_text":"Based on the log you've shared, I can see the biometric authentication succeeded at 11:35:03.928, but there are no explicit logs from your application showing what happens immediately after that success.

However, we can infer what's happening from the subsequent system logs:

1. At 11:35:03.959 - The system is handling UI elements for `MainActivity` (navigation bar and status bar)
2. At 11:35:03.961 - The `ViewRoot` for `MainActivity` is being updated
3. At 11:35:03.993-11:35:03.999 - The `ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity` is being stopped and its window is being destroyed

This sequence indicates that after biometric authentication succeeds:

1. The `onAuthenticationSucceeded` callback is triggered
2. Your code calls `finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)` which sends the result to the caller
3. The activity finishes and control returns to `MainActivity`

To better understand what's happening in your application after authentication, you should add logging in these key places:

````kotlin path=authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/ui/ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity.kt mode=EDIT
override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: AndroidXBiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
 super.onAuthenticationSucceeded(result)
 Log.i(TAG, "Authentication succeeded, sending RESULT_OK to caller")
 finishWithResult(Activity.RESULT_OK)
}

private fun finishWithResult(resultCode: Int) {
 Log.i(TAG, "Sending result code: $resultCode to ResultReceiver")
 resultReceiver?.send(resultCode, Bundle())
 finish()
}
````

Also, add logging in the `ResultReceiver` implementation in `AuthenticatorContext.kt` where you handle the authentication result:

````kotlin path=authenticator/src/main/java/me/henneke/wearauthn/fido/context/AuthenticatorContext.kt mode=EDIT
// In the confirmDeviceCredentialInternal method
val intent = Intent(context, ConfirmDeviceCredentialActivity::class.java).apply {
 putExtra(
 EXTRA_CONFIRM_DEVICE_CREDENTIAL_RECEIVER,
 object : ResultReceiver(Handler()) {
 override fun onReceiveResult(resultCode: Int, resultData: Bundle?) {
 Log.i(TAG, "Received authentication result: $resultCode")
 // Process the result here
 continuation.resume(null)
 }
 })
 // ...
}
````

This will help you track the flow of execution after biometric authentication succeeds.
